channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Wisconsin reporter gives insight on country’s most expensive judicial race
item.description: More than $90 million has poured into a Wisconsin Supreme Court race that will decide the court’s ideological majority.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: The Latest: Trump withdraws Elise Stefanik’s UN ambassador nomination over tight GOP House margin
item.description: President Donald Trump says protecting national security gives him the authority to end collective bargaining with labor unions across most of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dem Sen. Mark Kelly fires back after Musk stands by 'traitor' accusation
item.description: Democratic Sen. Mark Kelly of Arizona responds after Elon Musk stands by calling him a "traitor" for arguing that the U.S. should support Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance and wife to tour US military base in Greenland after diplomatic spat over uninvited visit
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — U.S. Vice President JD Vance and his wife are due to visit an American military base in Greenland on Friday in a trip that was scaled back after an uproar among Greenlanders and Danes who were irked that the original itinerary was planned without consulting them.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP CROWTHER and KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs executive order to end collective bargaining at agencies involved with national security
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump moved Thursday to end collective bargaining with federal labor unions in agencies with national security missions across the federal government, citing authority granted him under a 1978 law.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California Assembly Democrats announce plans to leave X over disinformation, hate speech
item.description: California Assembly Democrats announced plans to leave X over disinformation, lack of content moderation and hate speech.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota lawyers say increased immigration enforcement is filling courtrooms
item.description: It’s been just more than two months since the Trump Administration began implementing its plans to deport more people in the country without legal status. And the impact is being felt at the Fort Snelling Immigration Court.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Battle over proxy voting for new parents intensifies in House GOP
item.description: The battle between Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-Fla.) and House GOP leaders over looming floor action to allow proxy voting for new parents is intensifying as both sides of the heated debate dig in their heels — with the clash potentially coming to a head as soon as next week.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Maine school officials won't comply with Trump administration agreement to bar transgender athletes
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — School officials in Maine said Thursday they will not comply with a proposed agreement from President Donald Trump's administration that would bar transgender athletes from participating in girls' sports. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE senator tells outdoors group to 'go fish' after discovering massive grant tied to high salaries
item.description: Sen. Joni Ernst is trying to reel in a grant from the Interior Department to the RBFF, which the organization said is being spent responsibly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here are the international students and faculty known to be targeted by ICE
item.description: The Trump administration’s crackdown on foreign students and faculty who have voiced support for the Palestinian cause has escalated, with universities across the country seeing students either arrested by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or having their visas revoked.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Senate committee presses Pentagon watchdog for answers on Signal chat
item.description: Senate Armed Services Committee Chair Roger Wicker (R-Miss.) and ranking member Jack Reed (D-R.I.) have sent a letter to the Defense Department’s acting inspector general demanding more information about a Signal chat on which senior Trump officials apparently discussed military attack plans.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court halts Chutkan's order for DOGE records
item.description: A federal appeals court on Wednesday temporarily blocked an order from U.S. District Judge Tanya Chutkan directing Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to turn over records and answer questions about its efforts to slash the federal bureaucracy. A three-judge panel on the U.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Health and Human Services will lay off 10,000 workers and close agencies in a major restructuring
item.description: A Democratic senator says she's worried about the fallout from a major overhaul and layoffs at the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump administration potential travel ban concerning airlines: Editor
item.description: "Even the announcement that they're being proposed on the list is sending a chill through the travel industry.
item.dc:creator: Markie Martin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration says it will pull back billions in COVID funding from local health departments
item.description: Federal health officials said Tuesday they are pulling back $11.4 billion in COVID-19-related funds for local public health departments and other health organizations throughout the nation. Minnesota stands to lose $226 million.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press and Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers to make Trump White House visit next month
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers plan to visit President Donald Trump at the White House to celebrate their World Series victory, the team announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: FBI launches Tesla vandalism task force
item.description: The FBI confirmed Monday it has established a task force to investigate the wave of vandalism and arson incidents targeting Tesla in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Live updates: Ratcliffe, Gabbard pressed on war plans Signal group chat
item.description: The Trump administration is scrambling to contain the fallout from a bombshell Atlantic article revealing its editor-in-chief had been mistakenly included in a Signal group chat involving national security adviser Mike Waltz, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, intelligence leadership and other Trump ad
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5

item.title: Judge won't lift block on Trump use of Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge James Boasberg declined to lift a restraining order barring the Trump administration from using the Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans, determining migrants are likely to succeed in pushing for hearings on whether they are in a gang.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
